findings.message: Target PR #6 changes only the expected draft/staging app status files and does not add connector, deploy, credential, social, or automation code.
evidence: GitHub changed-file list returned README.md, package.json, src/data/templates.js, src/lib/safety.js, src/lib/status.js, src/main.js, and src/styles.css.

findings.message: The app now separates review result from owner decision: approved_draft remains the review outcome, while owner_approved_draft records the owner draft-content approval.
evidence: Direct data assertion confirmed ownerApprovedDraftCount 21, pendingDraftApprovalCount 0, weekly report approvalStatus owner_approved_draft, and all post package approvalStatus values owner_approved_draft.
src/lib/safety.js requires the sprint approval status, weekly report approval status, and all owner-approved package states while keeping reviewDecision approved_draft.

findings.message: Mandatory live-action blocks remain present after the draft approval state update.
evidence: All handles remain not_provided and pending_owner_input.
All accounts remain not_connected, draft_only, approvalRequired true, livePostingBlocked true, and credentialsStored false.
README.md and UI copy state that owner_approved_draft does not authorize scheduling, posting, social OAuth, account connection, analytics, DMs/comments, n8n activation, or credential storage.

findings.message: Safe target checks passed locally without dependency installation, live connector action beyond opening the PR, deployment, or external service calls.
evidence: GitHub reports PR #6 mergeable true and there are no target repo status checks configured.

optionalImprovements: After merge, redeploy only the dedicated Netlify staging-only site and record the new deploy result.
Keep handles pending_owner_input until the owner provides exact official handles.
Use the Social OAuth Readiness Package only as a future approval package; do not execute OAuth from this PR.
limitations: Critique is advisory only and cannot approve, merge, deploy, connect accounts, schedule, post, use credentials, call analytics APIs, activate automations, or create accepted lessons.
Review did not run browser visual regression or accessibility automation.
Review did not alter target repository settings, sensitive values, live social accounts, deployments, or domain/DNS.
